summary: QA accepts M5-DASHBOARD-OPPORTUNITY-LIST-001. The working tree adds the requested server-rendered dashboard and opportunity list pages, shared Prisma-backed serializable opportunity view-model helper, reusable OpportunityCard and ScoreBadge components, query-string filters, empty states, README documentation, and focused tests. Review found no blocking acceptance or product-scope issue.

acceptance_criteria_review.criterion: Dashboard page, persisted metrics, sorting, and Top 5 selection.
acceptance_criteria_review.notes: app/dashboard/page.tsx:8 defines the server page and app/dashboard/page.tsx:73 renders Today's Top 5. lib/opportunities.ts:214-229 queries persisted Opportunity/SearchRun counts and aggregate score; lib/opportunities.ts:310-311 sorts serialized opportunities and slices the first 5. Tie-breakers are deterministic by score desc, createdAt desc, id asc at lib/opportunities.ts:630-647.

acceptance_criteria_review.criterion: Opportunity list query-string filters and invalid parameter behavior.
acceptance_criteria_review.notes: app/opportunities/page.tsx:18 defines the list page. The form exposes radarTaskId, minScore, toolType, riskLevel, and status controls at app/opportunities/page.tsx:115,133,143,159,175. lib/opportunities.ts:480-497 normalizes aliases/invalid params; lib/opportunities.ts:559-579 pushes DB-supported filters into Prisma; lib/opportunities.ts:448-476 applies the full filter set, including derived risk.

lib/opportunities.ts:394-444 serializes Opportunity rows into plain props, encodes detailHref at lib/opportunities.ts:409, and converts Date fields to ISO strings at lib/opportunities.ts:440-444. tests/opportunities.test.ts:118-136 asserts JSON.stringify works for the serialized model.

components/ScoreBadge.tsx:18-47 renders clamped score badges. components/OpportunityCard.tsx:26-98 renders score, title/keyword, tool type, market, summary, monetization, risk, status, build complexity, and detail link. Risk levels are derived at lib/opportunities.ts:502-545, labeled at lib/opportunities.ts:551-556, styled for high/excluded at components/OpportunityCard.tsx:14-18, and always rendered at components/OpportunityCard.tsx:90-93.

acceptance_criteria_review.criterion: Empty states and restrained operational UI.
acceptance_criteria_review.notes: Dashboard empty states render at app/dashboard/page.tsx:98 and app/dashboard/page.tsx:156. Opportunity list empty state renders at app/opportunities/page.tsx:83. The pages use compact operational layouts without marketing hero content, nested cards, package changes, or global style changes.

tests/opportunities.test.ts:19,58,118,139,158,166 cover Top 5 ordering, combined filters and aliases, serializable view models, invalid params and empty list inputs, ScoreBadge rendering, and OpportunityCard risk/monetization/detail-link rendering.

README.md:290-319 documents the M5 pages, data dependency, filter parameters, validation commands, and non-goals. Working tree product changes are limited to allowed paths; project/task/message changes are JustGoal coordination state. No API, schema, scoring, services, agents, package, global style, app shell, or detail-route implementation was found.

tests_run.result: VALID active task JSON; validated 1 task.

Reviewed dirty tree: README.md and task JSON modified; new app/dashboard, app/opportunities, components, lib/opportunities.ts, implementation handoff, and tests/opportunities.test.ts. Product artifacts are within allowed paths.
PASS: no whitespace errors. Git printed line-ending conversion warnings for README.md and the active task JSON.
PASS: 6 focused tests passed across 2 suites.

Initial run compiled and generated pages but failed finalization with ENOENT renaming .next/export/500.html to .next/server/pages/500.html. Treated as transient local build artifact behavior and reran immediately.

PASS on immediate rerun: Next.js production build completed, including dynamic /dashboard and /opportunities routes. Existing nonfatal local @next/swc-win32-x64-msvc invalid Win32 application warnings were printed before fallback build continued.

decisions: ACCEPT: Acceptance criteria are met with no requested product-code changes.
Accepted derived risk filtering for this slice because risk is not a dedicated database column and risk cues are visible wherever opportunity cards render.
Accepted stable links to /opportunities/{id} without implementing the detail route, matching the task non-goal split with Task 13.
Accepted project/* task and message edits as JustGoal coordination state rather than product implementation scope expansion.
risks: Risk filtering is applied after fetching a bounded ordered set when riskLevel is present because risk is derived from rawAnalysis/riskSummary/scoreBreakdown rather than indexed as a scalar column; very large local datasets may need a later risk column or pagination.
Focused component tests assert medium-risk rendering, while high/excluded risk treatment is verified by code review rather than a dedicated high/excluded HTML assertion.
Top 5 currently ranks by score/date/id and clearly labels risky items if they appear; later ranking policy may still choose to hard-exclude high/excluded opportunities from dashboard Top 5.
The first npm run build attempt hit a transient .next finalization ENOENT before the immediate rerun passed.
Next build continues to print existing nonfatal local SWC native-package warnings.
requested_action: Integrate M5-DASHBOARD-OPPORTUNITY-LIST-001 after remaining required role reviews, if any. QA accepts the implementation with no requested changes.
